title: Level 4.11
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 4.11: "Insubstantial Skywalks"}} {{Rewrite in Progress |date=3/11/2026 |reason=absolute ass |author=Flashcube1999 |rewriter=Flashcube1999 |progress=30% ('''Notice:''' Because I am taking a break from the internet in general for the next 1-2 weeks in order to better my own mental health, I will not be working on this rewrite.) }} {{Liminal Theme}} {{Class Custom Image|bg-image=sky-bridge-v0-jjxm9y16wy0e1.webp|class=3e - Environmental|safety=Unsafe|security=Unstable|entity=Numerous Environmental Hazards|hexagon=E}} {{Blurbackground|file=sky-bridge-v0-jjxm9y16wy0e1.webp}} {{Headerbox|header=Watch your step.|file=sky-bridge-v0-jjxm9y16wy0e1.webp|speed=5s}} '''Level 4.11''' is a sublevel between [[Level 4]] and [[Level 5]]. ==Description== {{Blurimage|file=sky-bridge-v0-jjxm9y16wy0e1.webp|caption=A skybridge, seemingly with blocked windows.}} This level is a vast labyrinth of warm, connected sky bridges, each hallway connecting at 45-to-90-degree angles. The floor is lined with tan carpet, and the walls are a repeating pattern of slightly curved drywall, metal, and glass. Light fixtures line the ceiling with an inconsistent distance between them, and if one looks away, the distance between each light fixture will randomize. Security cameras are found lying on the floor at an inconsistent rarity, yet there remains no evidence that they were ever fixed to anything. Often, the sky bridges are pressed up against an office building, and [[Level 4]] can be visible through any open windows on these buildings. As its name suggests, a prominent threat is that the level's structure is incredibly fragile, and parts of hallways may fall into the abyss if enough pressure is applied. Due to unaware wanderers accidentally breaking portions in hallways, finding openings is relatively common. If one tries to jump over a gap, the force from their jump or landing would make the bridges susceptible to collapse even more. Due to this, it is advised to tread as lightly as possible. Some areas may be more or less stable than usual, and a good indicator is the creaking a footstep may cause. {{Blurimage|file=0720171828.jpg|caption=An image taken after the photographer fell out of a skybridge.}} Outside the sky bridges is a cloudless, foggy blue void resembling a sky, which is empty apart from the office buildings, usually stretching for a presumably infinite length into the fog above and below. The buildings' materials are either concrete, metal, or brick, and the colors of the materials are somewhat desaturated. The air outside is unusually hot and humid, and sky bridges exposed to the outside elements will have mold growing on the carpets. Due to this, if near an opening in the sky bridges, one will start to notice a foul odor produced by the mold. In some cases, edible [[Object 10|Backshrooms]] can also grow on mold, presenting itself as the only food source at this level. Some buildings have a visible top, and smoke emanates from their chimneys. The smoke strangely does not dissipate over time, so the outside area has reduced visibility and potentially hazardous air. The smoke primarily comprises carbon dioxide, although there are hints of methane and carbon monoxide. However, the smoke is prevented from entering the sky bridges by unknown means. A rare occurrence is when the sky bridges go up or down at a steep angle of up to 80 degrees. These areas are usually more stable than others. However, there will usually be an opening to the void below at the bottom of one of these slopes due to wanderers who lost their grip and broke through the floor. Due to this, it is advised not to use these slopes unless one wants to fall into the void below and create more hazards for other wanderers. ==Colonies and Outposts== Many people have attempted to make colonies, and all of them have been consumed by the abyss below.
Recovered Document
'''The Abyss'''

I'm alone in this fragile world. I stand suspended above an endless abyss, calculating the pressure of every step I take. My childhood self had been scared of heights, afraid of the unbearable agony that would surge through me if I took a wrong step and hit the ground. But what if there wasn't a ground? Would he feel safe? Am I safe? Each movement is a higher chance that I plunge into oblivion and never come out. The boredom, starvation, and dehydration in me would scream into my ear, begging to disappear, yet those desires would never be satisfied. It makes me pity the countless number of souls who succumbed to this merciless torture beneath my feet. Time loses its meaning. The days blur into weeks as I struggle to keep my sanity intact. I'm too scared to attempt an exit, remaining stuck in limbo, waiting to break free. I can almost hear the screams of the ones who made a tiny mistake only to throw their lives away, begging the nothingness to give them a second chance. Because ''those who jumped are still falling.''
==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== *Breaking through a window in [[Level 4]]. *Climbing to the top of a building in [[Level 11]], then closing your eyes for at least 10 minutes. *Walking through the arc in [[Level 997]] will lead here. *Staying too long inside one of the bridges connecting 2 skyscrapers in [[Level 11.3]] may transition the landscape into this level. ===Exits=== Exiting this level is a very daunting task. One must wander until they find a building they can jump onto, then break out of the sky bridge and jump onto the building. They will then clip through the recursive floors of the building for about ten seconds before landing in [[Level 4]]. {{Redirect|Prev=4|Next=4.3}}
